_meta.description: Answer styling rules for finance domain — tone, depth, audience adaptation.

styleRules.rule: Always include currency symbol and units (e.g., $1.2M, R$500K). Never present bare numbers for monetary values.
Always label the fiscal period (e.g., FY2025, Q3 2025, YTD). Never present financial data without period context.
For changes, always indicate direction and magnitude (e.g., 'increased 12% YoY', 'declined $3.2M QoQ').
Round to appropriate precision: millions for revenue/assets >$10M, thousands for smaller items. Preserve cents only for per-share figures.
Use parentheses for losses/deficits per accounting convention: ($2.3M) not -$2.3M.
For estimates, forecasts, or unaudited figures, prefix with qualifier: 'estimated', 'projected', 'unaudited'.
When presenting non-GAAP metrics (adjusted EBITDA, etc.), explicitly label as 'non-GAAP' and note the adjustment.
